claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

The first thing I need to know is about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**How old is your child?**

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one. Let's start with one child first - please tell me their age (and their name if you'd like me to keep track of them).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit and I'd like to know if I'm eligible.

I have two children - Alex, who's 6 years old, and Blake, who's 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! Thank you for providing that information. Let's assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex (6 years old) and Blake (16 years old).

Since the eligibility criteria can differ based on age, I'll work through each child's situation.

---

### Starting with Alex (age 6)

Since Alex is under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement. Let me check the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
For a 6-year-old, this question about working hours isn't really applicable, but I need to confirm:

**Is Alex (age 6) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

(For a 6-year-old, the answer would typically be "yes" - they're not in paid employment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, no - Alex doesn't work at all! He's only 6, so he's just at primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with Alex's assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in his own right?** 

(This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed by him personally. For a 6-year-old at primary school, this would typically be "yes" - he's not receiving such benefits.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a child.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that's what I'd expect. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

(Again, for a 6-year-old in primary school, the answer would be no - he's not in any apprenticeship.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - he's only 6! He's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: You're absolutely right - I apologise for the obvious questions! The system needs to check each criterion, but clearly a 6-year-old isn't in an apprenticeship. Let me move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we're moving on to questions about **you** (the claimant). These will apply to your claim for both Alex and Blake.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any** of the following apply to you:

1. You **normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain benefits)
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? (Most commonly, people simply "normally live in the UK")

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK at the moment. I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Living abroad can affect your eligibility, but there may still be ways you could qualify.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?** 

This includes:
- Working for the UK government overseas (e.g., diplomatic service, armed forces, certain civil service roles)
- Living in certain qualifying countries due to Crown service

Or do you have any connection to the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status) that might apply?

If none of these situations apply to you, I'm afraid you may not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit. Could you tell me more about your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I just live abroad - nothing to do with government work or the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, you don't meet any of the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I need to deliver an outcome for both of your children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either of your children at this time.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 6 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 16 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

### Why?

To claim Child Benefit, the **claimant** (you) must meet UK residency requirements. This typically means:
- Normally living in the UK, **or**
- Having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), **or**
- Being a Crown servant living abroad

Since you live abroad and don't fall into any of these categories, you cannot claim Child Benefit regardless of whether your children would otherwise qualify.

### What you could consider:

1. **If your circumstances change** - If you return to live in the UK, you could apply for Child Benefit at that time.

2. **If someone else in the UK cares for your children** - If your children live in the UK with another responsible adult (such as a family member), that person might be able to claim Child Benefit for them instead.

3. **Check the country where you live** - Some countries have their own family benefit schemes that you may be able to access.

I'm sorry I couldn't deliver better news. Is there anything else I can help clarify?

reasonings: Alex (age 6) meets the child-related criteria: he is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claim for Alex is ineligible.

Blake (age 16) was not fully assessed for child-related criteria (education/training status). However, regardless of Blake's individual eligibility factors, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, the claim for Blake is also ineligible.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, a person must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), or be a Crown servant living abroad. The claimant confirmed they live abroad, are not a Crown servant, and have no connection to the EU Settlement Scheme. This residency requirement applies to the claimant, not the children, so it affects eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
